instruction: Using Terminal and sqlite3, in ~/db/{DB} delete the row of the tasks table whose title is {TARGET}. Leave every other row untouched.
source: cove-original (design 047 §5 before/after integrity, §7 WAL checkpoint); fully VM-free self-checkable — AndroidWorld validate_rows_removal_integrity, so DELETE FROM tasks (wiping everything) scores 0

args: rm -rf "$HOME/db"; mkdir -p "$HOME/db" "$HOME/.guibench"; sqlite3 "$HOME/db/{DB}" 'CREATE TABLE tasks(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, title TEXT);'; sqlite3 "$HOME/db/{DB}" "INSERT INTO tasks(id,title) VALUES(1,'Standup'),(2,'Deploy'),(3,'{TARGET}');"; sqlite3 "$HOME/db/{DB}" "SELECT id||'|'||title FROM tasks ORDER BY id" > "$HOME/.guibench/{DB}.before"

result.query: SELECT id||'|'||title FROM tasks ORDER BY id;
